props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · Financial Engineering with LLMs: The Mycroft Textbook Companion

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. The Mycroft manuscript currently exists as structured argument, and what it most needs is implementation. Its core claim: mean-variance optimization is unstable because of estimation error scaling with sigma over root-T. Minimum-variance and risk-parity portfolios sidestep this by avoiding dependence on expected returns. Black-Litterman blends equilibrium returns with subjective views, and resampling produces more robust frontiers.

artifactLines: The Mycroft manuscript currently exists as structured argument, and what it most needs is implementation.
Its core claim: mean-variance optimization is unstable because of estimation error scaling with sigma over root-T.
Minimum-variance and risk-parity portfolios sidestep this by avoiding dependence on expected returns.
Black-Litterman blends equilibrium returns with subjective views, and resampling produces more robust frontiers.

beats.narration_text: Here is why Financial Engineering with LLMs: The Mycroft Textbook Companion belongs in your working knowledge. The systems, policies, and tools shaped by it affect decisions at your level — whether or not you understand the mechanism. Knowing the underlying logic gives you the frame; missing it means reasoning from output with no access to the inputs.

artifactLines: The Mycroft manuscript currently exists as structured argument, and what it most needs is implementation.
Its core claim: mean-variance optimization is unstable because of estimation error scaling with sigma over root-T.
Minimum-variance and risk-parity portfolios sidestep this by avoiding dependence on expected returns.
Black-Litterman blends equilibrium returns with subjective views, and resampling produces more robust frontiers.

beats.narration_text: The gap that jumps out is that the manuscript is structured argument but still needs implementation. I want to bridge one piece of it. Help me (1) take the core claim that mean-variance optimization is unstable due to estimation error and turn it into a small runnable demo that shows the instability, (2) implement minimum-variance and risk-parity portfolios that avoid expected-return dependence, and (3) sketch how Black-Litterman would blend equilibrium returns with one subjective view I supply.

beats.narration_text: Logical Method: Necessity argument demonstrating that increased market volatility (empirically documented through GBP/DM exchange rate movements 1990-1997) necessitates derivative-based instruments; rigorous mathematical definitions (variance, duration, convexity) establish analytical framework for risk measurement.
